claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 9 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have Alex, who is 9 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are specific rules that may apply to your situation.

Can you tell me which country you're currently living in, and whether you or your partner work as a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living abroad, but I'm not working as a Crown servant - I'm not a diplomat or in the armed forces, and neither is my partner.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Can you tell me which specific country you're currently living in? This is important because you may be able to get Child Benefit if you live in certain countries, even if you're not a Crown servant.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm currently living in Spain.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

One more important question: Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you living in Spain under different circumstances (for example, as a Spanish citizen, or you moved there from the UK)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I moved to Spain from the UK, but I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

To help determine your eligibility, I need to understand a bit more about your situation. Are you currently working for a UK employer or receiving a UK state pension, or do you have any other ongoing connection to the UK such as paying UK National Insurance contributions?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't work for a UK employer, I'm not receiving a UK state pension, and I'm not paying UK National Insurance contributions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also ask: Does Alex live with you in Spain?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. Alex lives back in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that important detail. 

Can you tell me who Alex is currently living with in the UK, and whether you are making regular financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living with their other parent in the UK. Yes, I do contribute regularly towards Alex's upkeep - I help with things like clothes, school costs, and general expenses. I send about £30 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know you're contributing £30 a week towards Alex's upkeep.

Just to clarify: Are you making these contributions under a court order or formal maintenance agreement, or is this an informal arrangement between you and Alex's other parent?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's an informal arrangement between me and Alex's other parent - there's no court order or formal maintenance agreement in place.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

One final question: Is Alex's other parent (who Alex lives with in the UK) currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or do you know if anyone else is claiming it?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I don't believe anyone is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The other parent hasn't mentioned claiming it.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## My Assessment

Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid **you are not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:

**For Alex (age 9):**
While Alex is under 16 (which meets the age requirement), the main issue is that **you must normally live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit**. You're currently living in Spain, and you don't meet the exceptions that would allow you to claim while living abroad.

The exceptions for living abroad include:
- Being a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces) - which you've confirmed you're not
- Meeting other specific criteria for certain countries

Although you're making regular financial contributions of £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which is commendable, this doesn't overcome the residency requirement when you're living abroad without the qualifying connections.

**What you could consider:**
- Alex's other parent (who lives with Alex in the UK) may be eligible to claim Child Benefit, as they live in the UK and are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, so if no one is currently claiming, it would be worth the other parent looking into making a claim.

I hope this clarifies your situation. If you have any questions, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant does not live in the UK - they live in Spain. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. The claimant may be able to get Child Benefit if they go to live in certain countries or if they're a Crown servant, but the claimant has confirmed they are not a Crown servant (not a diplomat or in HM Forces) and their partner is not either. While the claimant is making financial contributions of £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep, Alex does not live with the claimant - Alex lives with their other parent in the UK. The claimant is not working for a UK employer, not receiving a UK state pension, and not paying UK National Insurance contributions. Based on these circumstances, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit, as they live abroad (in Spain) without being a Crown servant or having the qualifying connections to the UK that would allow them to claim while living in certain countries.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in Spain (not in the UK) and is not a Crown servant. While they contribute £30 weekly towards their 9-year-old child Alex's upkeep, Alex lives with the other parent in the UK. The fundamental eligibility requirement is that the claimant must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit, unless they are a Crown servant or meet other specific criteria for living abroad in certain countries. Since the claimant does not meet these requirements, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

The fundamental eligibility requirement is that the claimant must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit, unless they are a Crown servant or meet other specific criteria for living abroad in certain countries. Since the claimant does not meet these requirements, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
